channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania-Comoros trade imbalance presents opportunities for exporters and investors
item.description: Dar es Salaam: Tanzania and Comoros are experiencing a significant trade imbalance that could become a driver for new business opportunities. From 2023 to 2025, Tanzania exported goods worth 48.85 mil...

item.title: Deputy President Opens 4IR Lab and Specialised Millwright Workshop at Gert Sibande TVET College
item.description: Deputy President Paul Mashatile, who also serves as Chairperson of the Human Resource Development Council, officially launched the Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) lab and the Centre of Specialisati...

item.title: Trump Pull Down AI Picture After Christian Leaders Call Am Blasphemy
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump don delete one AI-generated image wey e post for him own Truth Social account after many religious leaders and conservative Christians para say the post be blasphemy. The p...

item.title: Apple preparing display‑free AI glasses with multiple frame styles and oval camera to rival Meta Ray‑Bans
item.description: Apple is developing a line of display‑free AI smart glasses aimed at competing with Meta’s AI‑enabled Ray‑Ban glasses, according to reporting by Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman. The product is expected to be ...

item.title: Social Media Trends Influencing Travel Choices
item.description: Social media platforms like Instagram and TikTok are increasingly shaping where people choose to travel next. Many users turn to these platforms not just for entertainment, but for travel inspiration ...

item.title: US Farmers Face Rising Fertiliser Costs Amid Iran Conflict Disruptions
item.description: American farmers, particularly in North Carolina, are experiencing significant financial strain due to skyrocketing fertiliser prices following US-Israeli strikes on Iran. The military actions led to ...

item.title: CBC Investigation Reveals Continued Overcharging for Underweight Meat at Loblaw and Sobeys Stores
item.description: A recent CBC investigation has once again revealed that several Loblaw-owned and Sobeys-affiliated stores are selling underweight meat, resulting in overcharging customers. Despite previous assurances...

item.title: EKSU Launches ‘Digi-Teach’ Programme to Boost Digital Skills Among Lecturers and Students
item.description: For Ekiti State University (EKSU), digital learning don enter new level as Tunji Olowolafe, the vice-chancellor, don launch the ‘Digi-Teach’ programme. The initiative dey aim to make sure all 650 lect...

item.title: DCEA intensifies nationwide crackdown, confiscating nearly two tonnes of illegal drugs and arresting 77 suspects
item.description: In March 2026, Tanzania’s Drug Control and Enforcement Authority (DCEA) carried out extensive operations across the country that led to the seizure of almost two tonnes of illegal drugs and the destru...

item.title: BINI's Coachella Looks: A Spotlight on Filipino Design
item.description: Girl group BINI recently made waves at Coachella, and it wasn't just their performance that caught everyone’s attention – it was their outfits! The group's looks showcased the talent of Filipino desig...

item.title: Qantas reduces domestic flight schedule as jet fuel prices surge
item.description: Qantas, Australia’s national airline, has announced a significant reduction in its domestic flight services across the country, citing soaring jet fuel costs and ongoing global pressures linked to the...

item.title: Mayo Clinic Press: Genetic Testing Information Unavailable
item.description: This news article isn't a standard news piece; it's an error message encountered when attempting to access a specific resource on the Mayo Clinic Press website. The attempt to view an article titled '...

item.title: Sergeant Nkosi to face June trial for alleged evidence tampering in Limpopo cash‑in‑transit case
item.description: The National Prosecuting Authority (NPA) in Limpopo has confirmed that Sergeant Fannie Nkosi will stand trial in June over allegations that he tampered with police dockets linked to a major cash‑in‑tr...

item.title: CM Stalin campaigns door-to-door in Vellore ahead of Tamil Nadu assembly elections
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin has actively started his campaign for the forthcoming assembly elections by visiting Vellore in a door-to-door outreach programme. During his visits, he interacted...

item.title: Montreal Port Faces Leadership Changes Amid Executive Departures
item.description: The Montreal Port Authority is experiencing a significant leadership shake-up, raising concerns about its governance and future direction. Barely a week after CEO Julie Gascon unexpectedly resigned, t...

item.title: Nakuru's Night Economy Struggles Four Years After City Status
item.description: Nakuru, Kenya, a city that recently attained city status, is grappling with a significant decline in its nightlife scene, impacting the local economy and altering the character of the area. Once a vib...

item.title: Ex-bagman claims Mans Carpio asked help to fix magnetic lifter case linked to missing shabu
item.description: Ramil Madriaga, a man who allegedly served as the former bagman of Vice President Sara Duterte, testified before the House justice committee that Sara’s husband, Mans Carpio, once sought his assistanc...

item.title: Family of Megan Gallagher speaks after conviction of seven individuals involved in her death
item.description: In Saskatoon, the family of Megan Gallagher delivered their final statements in court following the conviction of seven individuals connected to her death. The emotional testimony focused on the immen...

item.title: Analysis of Increasing Hate Speech and Its Impact on Indian Society
item.description: In 2025, India witnessed a troubling rise in hate speech incidents, with the Centre for the Study of Organized Hate reporting 1,318 events targeting religious minorities across 21 states, one union te...

item.title: How a Kenyan entrepreneur turned snail farming into a profitable business
item.description: Back in 2017, Roussoss Demisse first saw snails as pests invading his mushroom farm in Kenya. Initially planning to get rid of them, he discovered through research that in West Africa, snails were act...
